title: Rubus godronii microgene grandibasis
taxonomy: Rubus godronii microgene grandibasis belongs to the family Rosaceae, which includes a wide variety of flowering plants such as roses and strawberries. The genus Rubus comprises over 600 species of bramble plants, many of which are edible or have ornamental value. As a specific microgene within the Rubus godronii species, grandibasis is a subspecies classification that indicates a specific variation within the larger species. The classification of Rubus godronii microgene grandibasis is a part of the scientific naming system that helps to categorize and understand the diversity of plant species. This classification system is based on the principles of binomial nomenclature, which was developed by Carl Linnaeus.
